title: The One Where Monica And Richard Are Jus

jokes.text: Six? I just had it for one night. It's three. / Eight o'clock's the cutoff. And, oh, it's 8:02.
jokes.setup: Customer expects standard late fee rules
jokes.punchline: Clerk smugly enforces arbitrary cutoff by mere minutes
jokes.explanation: Solid situational humor with good timing. The 8:02 specificity adds petty bureaucrat comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Hi, I'd like to return these videos. I know they're a few minutes late, but—

dialogue.speaker: Video store clerk
dialogue.line: A few minutes? Let me check the timestamp. You're past the cutoff by exactly three minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Three minutes? That's hardly late at all!

dialogue.speaker: Video store clerk
dialogue.line: Three minutes, three hours—doesn't matter. Late is late. That'll be forty-five dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Forty-five dollars?! For three minutes?!

dialogue.speaker: Video store clerk
dialogue.line: Store policy. Should've thought of that before you decided to watch the end of Seinfeld.

jokes.text: You know, in a weird way, you have too much power.
jokes.setup: Video clerk's petty authority over late fees
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about minor power dynamics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that elevates the mundane situation. Good character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Video Clerk
dialogue.line: You know, people come in here all the time trying to negotiate their late fees. They think they're special. But I have the power here. Me. The video clerk.

dialogue.speaker: Video Clerk
dialogue.line: I can waive them or enforce them. I can make their day or ruin it. It's intoxicating, really.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: It's just a late fee.

dialogue.speaker: Video Clerk
dialogue.line: Exactly. That's what makes it beautiful. In the real world, nobody listens to me. But here? Here I am God.

jokes.text: Your lip went bald.
jokes.setup: Richard appears without his mustache
jokes.punchline: Monica describes mustache removal as baldness
jokes.explanation: Clever, unexpected description. Perfect economy and originality in framing facial hair as 'baldness'.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Richard! What happened to your mustache?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I shaved it off. What do you think?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You look... bald.

jokes.text: You got panties stuck to your leg.
jokes.setup: Awkward encounter between exes trying to be casual
jokes.punchline: Static cling reveals underwear stuck to Monica's clothes
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup. Good integration of embarrassing moment during awkward reunion.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Monica, is that...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: You have, um... something stuck to your sweater.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Static cling.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is so embarrassing.

jokes.text: Or maybe God knew I'd be running into you and saw an opportunity.
jokes.setup: Monica's practical explanation for static cling
jokes.punchline: Turns mundane laundry mishap into divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurotic overthinking. Escalates from practical to cosmic in believable character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's just static cling. Throw the clothes back in the dryer with a damp cloth for a few minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Or, hear me out, God is trying to tell us something about our laundry habits.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not how static cling works.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's not how God works either, but here we are.

jokes.text: He's going to the counter with Citizen Kane, Vertigo and Clockwork Orgy.
jokes.setup: Guy browsing classics will secretly rent porn
jokes.punchline: Porn title parody of 'A Clockwork Orange'
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup with perfect punchline. The porn title parody is clever and memorable. Classic Chandler observational humor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A Clockwork Orange? More like A Clockwork Orgy!

jokes.text: I think it's too soon. / No, it's not too soon. I had lunch at 11.
jokes.setup: Monica worried about emotional timing of seeing Richard
jokes.punchline: Richard thinks she's talking about meal timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with clean structure. Shows character differences through miscommunication.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know if I'm ready to see you. The timing just feels... I mean, emotionally, I'm not sure I can handle this right now.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Well, what time works better for you? I can do dinner next Tuesday or Thursday.

jokes.text: No, thanks. I'm 29.
jokes.setup: Joey offers chocolate milk
jokes.punchline: Chandler's age as reason to decline kid's drink
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with economical delivery. Mild but effective age-based humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, you want some chocolate milk?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No thanks, I'm too old for that.

jokes.text: Every night that you're asleep before I get home... I will wake you up in a way that has proved popular in the past.
jokes.setup: Chandler apologizing for working late
jokes.punchline: Sexual euphemism disguised as romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Clever innuendo with formal phrasing making it funnier. Good Chandler voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry I worked late. Let me make it up to you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I was thinking I could take you to that new French restaurant you wanted to go to.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Or we could just stay in and I could give you a little oral pleasure.

jokes.text: Well, if you need to stay late, I want to be supportive of that.
jokes.setup: Chandler's sexual bribe for working late
jokes.punchline: Monica's immediate attitude change about his schedule
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Monica's instant priority shift. Solid but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're working late? That's so inconsiderate! You know I hate eating dinner alone.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, I'm sorry. But my boss really needs me to finish this project.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you just... work from home?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't work from home.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh. Well then, you should definitely stay as late as you need to. That's very important. Your career is so sexy.

jokes.text: And what are you gonna do for him? / I'm gonna let him.
jokes.setup: Exchange of favors for athletic instruction
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's passive but suggestive response
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline structure with mild innuendo. Functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so you'll teach me guitar and I'll teach you aerobics?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay. But I'm gonna need you to be very... hands on.

jokes.text: Around 8:02.
jokes.setup: Asked when she ran into Richard
jokes.punchline: References the earlier video store late fee time
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that connects scenes. Good economy and rewards attentive viewers.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: When I ran into him at the video store. He owed me a late fee.

jokes.text: There's no such thing as an 'innocent burger'.
jokes.setup: Monica claims innocent meal with ex
jokes.punchline: Rachel's worldly wisdom about ex encounters
jokes.explanation: Quotable line with good insight into dating dynamics. Strong economy and memorable phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, but Monica, you can't just have an innocent meal with an ex. That's like saying you can have an innocent shower with someone.

jokes.text: You might want to make extra, because you'll be hungry after the sex.
jokes.setup: Monica planning to teach Richard cooking
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt prediction about inevitable outcome
jokes.explanation: Direct, blunt humor that cuts through Monica's self-deception. Good character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, honey, you're going to end up sleeping with him.

jokes.text: Naked friends.
jokes.setup: Monica insisting they'll just be friends
jokes.punchline: Chandler's two-word reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - maximum impact with minimum words. Classic Chandler cutting through BS.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're not.

jokes.text: If there's none in there, we're probably out.
jokes.setup: Monica asks Chandler to check freezer for ice
jokes.punchline: Chandler's unnecessarily obvious logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler smartass response. Good character voice with absurd logic.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Can you check the freezer for ice?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, let me think about this logically. Ice is cold. The freezer is cold. So yes, there's probably ice in the freezer.

jokes.text: I discovered I'm able to count all my teeth using just my tongue.
jokes.setup: Contrast with Rachel's professional Calvin Klein meeting
jokes.punchline: Joey's pointless but impressive personal discovery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character beat. Absurd achievement delivered with pride. Great contrast to Rachel's sophistication.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I just realized something. I can fit my entire fist in my mouth.

jokes.text: I was reading it last night and I got scared, so...
jokes.setup: Rachel finds 'The Shining' in freezer
jokes.punchline: Joey's childlike fear management strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Joey's childlike logic. Visual gag of book in freezer plus innocent explanation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, why is 'The Shining' in the freezer?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, I got scared watching it, so I figured if I freeze it, maybe it'll chill out.

jokes.text: But you're safe from it if it's in the freezer? / Well, safer.
jokes.setup: Rachel questions freezer protection logic
jokes.punchline: Joey's unwavering but slightly uncertain belief
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing Joey's commitment to his illogical system. 'Safer' shows he's not 100% sure but sticking with it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me that if I put myself in the freezer, I won't age?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's right. It's science.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not how it works.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm pretty sure it does. I saw it in a movie once. Or... I think I did.

jokes.text: I never start reading it... without making sure we got room in the freezer.
jokes.setup: Joey's freezer protection established
jokes.punchline: Reveals this is a systematic, repeated practice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - not just a one-time thing but a whole system. Shows Joey's deep commitment to his irrational fear management.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I got a system. Every time I buy ice cream, I put a picture of myself on the box.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So when I open the freezer and see my face, I think, 'Oh, that's Joey's ice cream. I can't eat that.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Does it work?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No! I just think he's not gonna mind.

jokes.text: How little are they? I mean, are they, like, scary little?
jokes.setup: Joey agrees to read 'Little Women'
jokes.punchline: Joey interprets 'little' as potentially threatening size
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey interpretation of book title through his horror-obsessed lens. Character-driven misunderstanding.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I read that book you told me about. Little Women.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But here's the thing - I'm not really comfortable with it. What if they're like, dangerously small? Like, what if they turn on you?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, little things can be vicious, man. You ever see a small dog? Tiny but mean.

jokes.text: That's okay, it's mine. We just kissed.
jokes.setup: Someone points out Phoebe has lipstick on her
jokes.punchline: Phoebe clarifies she's wearing her own lipstick
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection moment with Phoebe's literal interpretation. Solid but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's my own lipstick!

jokes.text: Right. Sometimes you guys just burst into flame.
jokes.setup: Robert from California wearing shorts in cold weather
jokes.punchline: Chandler's absurd explanation for California weather adaptation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler absurdist humor. Takes California stereotype to ridiculous extreme with perfect delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you see, in California the sun is closer to the earth. So your legs get used to the heat, and then when you come to New York, your legs don't know what hit them. It's like when you go from a tanning bed directly into a snowstorm.

jokes.text: He's coming out of his shorts.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Robert is 'coming out' as gay
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifies the literal clothing issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on 'coming out' with perfect misdirection setup. Creates ongoing visual comedy situation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Is Robert coming out?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, he's coming out of the closet. Literally. His clothes are stuck.

jokes.text: The man is showing brain.
jokes.setup: Need to explain Robert's exposure problem
jokes.punchline: Chandler's euphemistic way of describing male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Exceptional euphemism that's both clever and anatomically descriptive. Perfect Chandler voice with maximum economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could he BE any more exposed?

jokes.text: Like an eclipse.
jokes.setup: Should avoid looking directly at Robert's exposure
jokes.punchline: Ross compares it to dangerous solar phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comparison elevating awkward situation to cosmic event. Perfect economy and unexpectedly sophisticated metaphor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You can't look directly at it. It's like a solar eclipse.

jokes.text: You touch people's eyeballs and this feels weird?
jokes.setup: Richard (eye doctor) finds tomato squishing weird
jokes.punchline: Monica points out the irony given his profession
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony showing Monica's logical mind. Solid professional reference humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I find it ironic coming from a man who spends all day looking at dilated pupils.

jokes.text: That's my hand.
jokes.setup: Intimate tomato squishing activity
jokes.punchline: Awkward moment of contact during cooking lesson
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy moment with mild innuendo. Functional but predictable setup for romantic tension.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So you just squish the tomatoes like this...

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Like this?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's... that's good. Your hands are very close to mine.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: I'll just throw them out.
jokes.setup: Getting tomato sauce on clothes during cooking
jokes.punchline: Richard's extreme solution to minor stain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing wealthy character's casual attitude toward expensive clothes. Economic delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: A tomato sauce stain? We need to burn the entire shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Richard, it's just a little spot.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: A little spot today is a big stain tomorrow. We must act decisively.

jokes.text: Jo's a girl. It's short for Josephine. / But Jo's got a crush on Laurie.
jokes.setup: Joey confused about Little Women characters' genders
jokes.punchline: Joey persists in thinking it's a gay relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Joey's confusion about classic literature. Sets up further misunderstanding.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, so Jo is a girl?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes, Joey. Jo is a girl.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And Laurie?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Also a girl.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So... it's not a love story?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It is a love story, Joey. Jo and Laurie—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, I get it. It's a love story. I'm just saying, good for them.

jokes.text: That is the one thing missing from 'The Shining'.
jokes.setup: Thinking Little Women has lesbian content
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests adding it to his favorite horror story
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist callback combining Joey's genre confusion with his Shining obsession. Perfect character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, Little Women has lesbian content? That's awesome! We should add it to my favorite horror story.

jokes.text: No wonder Rachel had to read this so many times.
jokes.setup: Learning Laurie is actually male
jokes.punchline: Joey assumes Rachel was confused like him
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Joey's assumption that everyone shares his confusion. Logical from his perspective.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, Laurie's a guy?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, didn't you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No! I thought... I mean, I was so confused!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, were you confused too?

jokes.text: You mean a 3-pointer? / I get more because I'm dainty.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claims to have shot a 23-pointer in basketball
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logic for getting extra points
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic creating her own scoring system. Absurd but delivered with complete confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I shot a 23-pointer.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, the basket is only worth 3 points.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I was standing really far away, and I had to throw it really hard, so I think that should count for more.

jokes.text: I always carry one in my sock.
jokes.setup: Offer of a quarter for phone
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unusual but practical storage method
jokes.explanation: Mild Phoebe quirk showing her unconventional but practical approach. Character-appropriate oddness.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I keep a quarter in my butt at all times.

jokes.text: Calm down. No reason to get testy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting upset about friends' behavior around Robert
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pun on 'testy' given Robert's exposure situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun perfectly timed for the situation. Classic Chandler wordplay with anatomical reference.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'd be testy too if my testicles were hanging out.

jokes.text: It's a word.
jokes.setup: Monica uses the made-up word 'platonomy'
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive assertion of her neologism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's intellectual defensiveness. Economical but mild impact.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's a real word! Platonomy. It's the study of platonic relationships.

jokes.text: Like racquetball.
jokes.setup: Describing casual sex arrangement with Richard
jokes.punchline: Monica's sanitized sports analogy
jokes.explanation: Good euphemism showing Monica's need to make everything sound organized and healthy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's like we're on the same team, but we're not playing for the same trophy.

jokes.text: Just your dad.
jokes.setup: Monica asks about other 'racquetball' partners
jokes.punchline: Richard mentions Monica's father
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with uncomfortable implications. Great economy and timing for maximum awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Have you had other racquetball partners?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Oh, a few over the years. Your father was one of them, actually.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My father?

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Yes, we played quite a bit before... well, before things got complicated.

jokes.text: The greatest things in the world! If I were you, I'd wear them every day.
jokes.setup: Phoebe gives Robert stretchy pants as a gift
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's enthusiastic promotion of practical clothing
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate gift-giving with mild irony. Sets up the shorts situation resolution but weak impact.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: They're stretchy! You can eat a big lunch, then your pants just grow with you. No more undoing your pants at the table like some kind of animal.

jokes.text: They're so tight. I'd feel like I'm on display.
jokes.setup: Robert declining stretchy pants gift
jokes.punchline: Robert's concern about being 'on display' while already exposed
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Robert's obliviousness to his current display situation. Solid callback payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: I appreciate the gesture, but I can't accept these.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: If I wear stretchy pants, I'll be on display. And frankly, I'm already exposed enough as it is.

jokes.text: All 'blank' and no 'blank' makes 'blank' a 'blank-blank'.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to avoid spoiling The Shining
jokes.punchline: Attempting to quote famous line in code
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about spoiler prevention, though execution is somewhat clunky. Creative concept.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I have to tell you something about The Shining, but I promised I wouldn't spoil it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So... here's the thing. All work and no play makes Jack a... uh... makes Jack a very dull boy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, that's literally the whole plot.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No it's not! I was being cryptic!

jokes.text: I can't believe she cracked your code.
jokes.setup: Joey understands Rachel's obvious 'code'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic amazement at the 'difficulty'
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm pointing out the obviousness of Rachel's 'code'. Solid but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wow, that's a tough code to crack. I mean, who would've thought 'I don't want to see you anymore' meant she didn't want to see him anymore?

jokes.text: Mine was by accident!
jokes.setup: Spoiler war between Rachel and Joey
jokes.punchline: Rachel's defensive justification for her spoiler
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in spoiler war showing Rachel's competitive side. Functional but mild impact.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I was just trying to help! If you know what's going to happen, you can prepare yourself emotionally.

jokes.text: Because I wanted to hurt you.
jokes.setup: Joey asking why Rachel would spoil Beth's death
jokes.punchline: Rachel's brutally honest motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy. Rachel's honest cruelty delivered with devastating simplicity.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because I'm jealous! She gets to die and leave the show, and I'm stuck here doing this forever!

jokes.text: I lost one of my fake boobs in a grill fire.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her terrible day
jokes.punchline: Bizarre workplace accident with prosthetic
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment with specific, unexpected detail. Memorable and quotable workplace mishap.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You won't believe the day I had at the restaurant. There was this bizarre workplace accident with a prosthetic limb that fell into the soup station, and then—

jokes.text: I love this 'friend' thing!
jokes.setup: Monica and Richard's casual arrangement
jokes.punchline: Monica's enthusiastic endorsement after sex
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Monica's post-coital satisfaction with their arrangement. Predictable but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That was amazing! You're like a Porsche and every other guy I've been with is like a golf cart.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Well, I have had more practice.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, keep practicing.

jokes.text: Yeah, I can spot you from here.
jokes.setup: Robert suggesting they spot each other rock climbing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pun about seeing Robert's exposure
jokes.explanation: Good double-meaning pun on 'spotting'. Classic Chandler wordplay with perfect callback timing.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: I'll spot you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more exposed?

jokes.text: Why does this keep happening? Is it something I'm putting out there?
jokes.setup: Robert confused about men hitting on him
jokes.punchline: Robert's obliviousness to what he's literally putting out there
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony with Robert's 'putting out there' while literally exposing himself. Perfect oblivious character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: I don't understand why men keep hitting on me. I'm not even gay.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, maybe it's something you're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: What do you mean? I'm just being myself.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but what are you wearing?

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: I don't know, clothes. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Robert, you're standing in the middle of the coffee shop wearing a tank top that says 'Ask Me' with an arrow pointing down, holding a cosmopolitan.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: So?

jokes.text: Hey, buddy? This is a family place. Put the mouse back in the house.
jokes.setup: Need to address Robert's exposure tactfully
jokes.punchline: Gunther's creative euphemism for the situation
jokes.explanation: Outstanding euphemism that's both creative and clear. Perfect resolution to the running gag with memorable phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Well, you see, Robert... what we have here is a situation where your... genitalia have become acquainted with the general public.

jokes.text: What if we're friends... who don't see other people?
jokes.setup: Monica realizing casual arrangement isn't working
jokes.punchline: Monica's step toward relationship without admitting it
jokes.explanation: Good character progression showing Monica's inability to maintain casual relationship. Functional escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? I think we should see other people.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because I think we're ready to take this to the next level.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, we're already living together.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, but... I want us to be exclusive. I want to stop seeing other people.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But we already are exclusive.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, but I want to... officially be exclusive. Like, we should tell people.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We've been telling people for months.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I mean... I want to be your girlfriend. For real this time. Not just casually.

jokes.text: Or maybe someday, friends who stood up in front of their other friends... and vowed to be friends forever.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting exclusive friendship
jokes.punchline: Monica's roundabout description of marriage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's inability to avoid relationship terminology. Clever euphemistic description of marriage.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? I think we should be each other's soulmates. And then we can just find men we can settle for.

jokes.text: That's not true. You don't have a moustache.
jokes.setup: Richard says nothing has changed between them
jokes.punchline: Monica points out the one visible change
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier mustache joke. Shows Monica's practical mind focusing on observable changes.

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: Nothing has changed between us.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, you've gone completely gray.

jokes.text: Watch the thorns!
jokes.setup: Richard leaving with roses
jokes.punchline: Monica's warning about the thorns on flowers
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag showing Monica's protective instinct. Functional but low impact ending moment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Be careful with those roses, the thorns are really sharp.

jokes.text: You wanna put the book in the freezer?
jokes.setup: Joey getting emotional about Beth's illness
jokes.punchline: Rachel offers Joey's own coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Rachel's understanding of Joey's methods. Perfect emotional callback timing for episode end.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you know what always makes you feel better?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Sleeping with someone.

tmdb_overview: Monica's willpower is tested when she runs into Richard at the video store. Rachel agrees to read The Shining if Joey reads Little Women. Phoebe's athletic date has a problem with short shorts.
insight: Monica-Richard dynamic carries 74 points despite modest 3.35 jokes-per-minute pace.
